title: Genius Intelligence
wikitext:
[[File:Shiro_card_edit.jpg|thumb|280px|[[Shiro (No Game No Life)|Shiro]] is a genius, exceptionally skilled in calculating and processing information]]

==Summary==
A '''genius''' is an individual with an exceptional capacity for knowledge and intelligence. Geniuses can appear in a variety of forms and with a variety of specialties, and their exceptional intelligence is not necessarily universal, potentially only applying to certain subjects and fields that they excel in. These can be any number of things, such as scientific fields, military tactics, martial arts, and/or investigation. There are many levels of genius, as well, detailed on the [[intelligence]] page; a regular genius, on the level of those that exist in the real world, is generally limited to a few areas of expertise, while fictional individuals of greater intellects may be masters of many, if not all fields of science and even strategy as well.

==Examples==
'''Geniuses:''' [[L (Death Note)|L]] and [[Light Yagami]] ([[Death Note]]), [[Solid Snake]] ([[Metal Gear]]), [[Shikamaru Nara]] and [[Kakashi Hatake]] ([[Naruto (Verse)|Naruto]]), [[Colress]] ([[Pokémon]])

'''Extraordinary Geniuses:''' [[Dexter (Dexter’s Laboratory)|Dexter]] ([[Dexter's Laboratory]]), [[Kisuke Urahara]] and [[Sosuke Aizen]] ([[Bleach]]), [[Batman (Post-Crisis)|Batman]] ([[DC Comics]]), [[Bulma]], [[Doctor Gero]], and [[Android 21]] ([[Dragon Ball]]), [[Spider-Man (Marvel Comics)|Spider-Man]], [[Hulk (Marvel Comics)|Bruce Banner]], [[Iron Man (Marvel Comics)|Iron Man]], and [[Thanos (Marvel Comics)|Thanos]] ([[Marvel Comics]]), [[Ozymandias (DC Comics)|Ozymandias]] ([[DC Comics|Watchmen]])

'''Supergeniuses:''' [[Brainiac (Post-Crisis)|Brainiac]] ([[DC Comics]]), [[The Doctor (Doctor Who)|The Doctor]] ([[Doctor Who]]), [[Doctor Doom]] and [[Mister Fantastic]] ([[Marvel Comics]]), [[Washu|Washu Hakubi]] ([[Tenchi Muyo!]]), [[The Emperor of Mankind]] ([[Warhammer 40,000]]), [[Rick Sanchez]] ([[Rick & Morty]])
